turns.speaker: Buyer
turns.text: Hi — I’m reaching out because the headphones arrived with a crack in the left earcup.

turns.speaker: Seller
turns.text: Sorry to hear that. Can you share a photo of the damage?

turns.speaker: Buyer
turns.text: Yes. I’d like a full refund including shipping. This is really disappointing.

turns.speaker: Seller
turns.text: I can offer a partial refund, but returns after 14 days are normally not accepted.

turns.speaker: Buyer
turns.text: That’s unfair — it arrived broken. If we can’t resolve this I may file a chargeback.

turns.speaker: Mediator
turns.text: Let’s slow down. Seller, can you confirm whether the item was insured in shipping? Buyer, would a replacement or a refund both be acceptable options?

turns.speaker: Seller
turns.text: It was insured. I can do a replacement shipped today, or a full refund if the buyer returns it.

turns.speaker: Buyer
turns.text: If you cover return shipping, I’m okay with returning it for a full refund.

turns.speaker: Mediator
turns.text: Great — agreement: Buyer returns item with prepaid label; Seller issues full refund upon scan acceptance.
